candidates.evidence_quote: we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $31.4 million primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets

In fiscal 2023, we recorded $29.5 million of expense primarily related to the impairment of three The Cheesecake Factory (one previously impaired), one North Italia (previously impaired), one Other FRC and two Other restaurant lease terminations.
We recorded $11.7 million and $13.4 million of expense during fiscal 2023 and 2022, respectively, of acquisition-related contingent consideration, compensation and amortization.
Preopening costs were $25.4 million for fiscal 2023 compared to $16.8 million for fiscal 2022.

We plan to open as many as 22 new restaurants in fiscal 2024, including three The Cheesecake Factory restaurants, six to seven North Italia restaurants, six to seven Flower Child locations and six to seven restaurants within our Other FRC business.
Based on recent trends and assuming no material operating or consumer disruptions, we anticipate total revenue for fiscal 2024 to be approximately $3.6 billion.
Including the 2.5% February 2024 price increase, we will have 4.5% pricing in The Cheesecake Factory menu, and we will evaluate the level of pricing for the summer menu based on inflationary trends.

we currently estimate total inflation across our commodities, total labor (factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and channel mix, as well as in other components such as payroll taxes and benefits) and other operating costs and expenses to be in the low to mid-single digit range
We anticipate commodity inflation to be in the low-single digit range and expect labor inflation to be in the mid-single digit range.
we expect fiscal 2024 net income margin of approximately 4.25% at the estimated revenue.
We anticipate approximately $180 to $200 million in cash capital expenditures to support this level of unit development, as well as required maintenance on our restaurants.

This increase was primarily due to higher interest on our Revolver Facility ($3.5 million), partially offset by higher interest income due to higher cash balances in interest bearing accounts ($1.1 million).
In fiscal 2023, we had an income tax benefit of $1.3 million, an effective tax rate of (1.3%), compared to an income tax benefit of $10.2 million, an effective tax rate of (31.1%), in fiscal 2022.
The change was due primarily to a smaller proportion of employment credits in relation to income before income taxes (49.4%)

Cash flows from operations increased by $56.5 million from fiscal 2022 primarily due to higher net income, a higher payroll accrual due to the timing of payments and higher incentive compensation accrued in fiscal 2023

Common stock dividends of $53.2 million and $42.3 million were paid in fiscal 2023 and 2022, respectively. The increase is primarily due to the resumption of our quarterly dividend in the second quarter of fiscal 2022
We repurchased 1.4 million shares at a cost of $46.1 million, excluding excise tax during fiscal 2023 compared to 2.0 million shares at a cost of $63.1 million fiscal 2022.

Under the Revolver Facility, we are subject to financial covenants, as well as to customary events of default that, if triggered, could result in acceleration of the maturity of the Revolver Facility.
The impact global and domestic economic conditions have on consumer discretionary spending and our costs of operations could materially adversely affect our financial performance.

Any factor affecting consumer discretionary spending may influence customer traffic in our restaurants and average check amount, thus potentially having a material impact on our financial performance.
We strive to increase comparable restaurant sales by improving customer traffic trends and growing average check.
Our inability to grow comparable restaurant sales could materially adversely affect our financial performance.
disruptions in the supply chain and delay in new restaurant openings
interest rates (including recent interest rates above historical norms)
result in reduced customer traffic and unfavorable menu mix shifts (i.e. customers reducing their spend by purchasing fewer menu items or lower cost menu items)
we have generated a higher mix of sales from off-premise channels as consumers have demonstrated a preference for convenience and at-home dining
a change in customers’ willingness to pay fees associated with third-party delivery could negatively impact our comparable restaurant sales performance
We compete directly and indirectly for customer traffic with national and regional casual dining restaurant chains, as well as independently-owned restaurants.
If we are unable to protect our reputation, the value of our brands and sales at our restaurants may be negatively impacted, which could materially adversely affect our financial performance.
Total revenues for the first quarter of fiscal 2024 are expected to be between $875 million to $895 million, including the impact from the inclement weather quarter-to-date.
Restaurant opening dates may be impacted by supply chain challenges and permit approval delays.
three Other restaurants that are primarily located in areas which have not fully recovered from the pandemic

notes: Chunk has 1 event: 10-K 0001104659-24-027565 part 3/6 (MD&A tail incl. fiscal 2024 outlook, liquidity/capital resources, critical accounting estimates, and start of Risk Factors). fiscal_kpis was null, so no KPI rewrites.
All 32 evidence quotes verified verbatim against source content via substring check before writing.
same_store_sales coined per R5/R6 standard phrase; source wording is 'comparable restaurant sales' (same canonical cause).
restaurant_unit_development named from the source's own 'unit development' wording because 'opening'/'growth' are banned name tokens.

Skipped as vague/laundry-list/no-change (16): goodwill_impairment (methodology only, no charge recorded), staffing challenges, geopolitical instability, unemployment, consumer confidence, credit conditions, stock market performance, home values, tax policy, consumer eating habits, G&A expense (guided flat), interest income (minor offset), convertible notes terms, operating lease obligations table, bakery production facility commitment footnote, incentive compensation timing.
labor_inflation deduped with wage-rate trend mentions; commodity_inflation deduped across outlook paragraphs (both backed by distinct quotes).
